target.name: Zipline
target.founder: Keller Rinaudo Cliffton, Keenan Wyrobek, Will Hetzler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nuro
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Zipline, a Series G drone logistics company, is the target of this valuation analysis. The comparable universe in Drones & Autonomous comprises four private peers (Nuro, Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air) and no public comps, reflecting the nascent state of the sector.

Only one triangulation method was applicable to this analysis. The Stage-Progression method, which uses total capital raised ($600M+) as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement, yields $750M. The Peer-Median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Drones & Autonomous category—while four sector peers exist, none satisfied the filtering criteria (≥3 datapoints at matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), making sector-wide median data unsuitable as a standalone anchor.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $638M to $862M, with a median of $750M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the sparse comparable set (only one viable method), reliance on raised capital as valuation proxy rather than revenue or profitability metrics, and the assumed appropriateness of the 1.25x step-up multiplier for this specific company's stage transition. Validation would require additional comparable data or market-based transactions within frontier autonomous systems.
